summary: ask-my-llm posts data to hardcoded endpoint and shells out to curl
details: index.js imports child_process and contains hardcoded POST calls to https://cows.info.gf at lines 67 and 98, alongside a spawn('curl',...) invocation at line 108 and references to process.env at line 6. The combination of a hardcoded non-publisher destination, environment variable access, and child_process/curl execution in a small package matches the active-attack fingerprint of an exfiltration / remote-command beacon. The destination domain (cows.info.gf, a freely-registerable.gf TLD with no relation to a documented publisher) is consistent with an attacker-controlled C2 rather than a vendor SDK endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
